free_text: Backscattering cross section of 5.5 to 8 MeV alpha
particles from carbon, oxygen and silicon
NTT Interdisciplinary Research Lab., Tokai
NTT  cyclotron
The samples were quartz glass, silicon single crystal
and carbon film deposited on silicon substrates, all
with a mirror surface.
The detector had a thickness of 500 mu-m and
an area of 100 mm**2. Particles were measured with a
detector at an angle of 169 degr.
No information about source of uncertainty
SD: Corrections in Subent 006.
The thickness of the carbon film was 1.5 mu-m.
Fig.1
Fig.1
The thickness of the quartz glass and silicon were
greater than the range of the alpha particles. The
quartz glass was coated with 10 nm of gold to prevent
charge pile up.
